dnd.invalidDropDesc: A block from category '{{category}}' cannot be placed in this zone.

stage1.title: Stage 1: Global Filters
stage1.desc: When should the strategy be active at all?

stage2.title: Stage 2: Position Entry
stage2.desc: What specific signal (setup) are we looking for?

stage3.title: Stage 3: Trade Initialization
stage3.desc: How exactly do we open the position?

stage4.title: Stage 4: Position Management
stage4.desc: What to do while the trade is open?

dropZone.actions: Drag action blocks here or add one below...

canvas.tradingViewWebhookHelpText: Entry conditions are provided by TradingView webhook events. Internal trigger and foundation blocks are bypassed in this mode.
canvas.tradingViewWebhookAlert: Webhook mode does not use manual entry condition blocks.
canvas.complexRiskWarning: In DCA/GRID mode, this risk applies to the FIRST order. The total grid will utilize more funds from your balance.
canvas.slDcaWarning: Warning! Your Stop Loss will trigger before the full DCA grid is executed (Max Drop: {{drop}}%). Increase SL or set to 0 (No SL).

configPanel.descriptionPlaceholder: A brief description of the strategy's logic...
configPanel.foundationWeightsTooltip: When activated, the root logic for Entry Conditions switches to 'OR'. The signal will trigger if the sum of weights of the met conditions exceeds the threshold. When deactivated, the logic is 'AND', and ALL conditions must be met.
configPanel.noFoundationsAdded: No foundations added to the strategy.
configPanel.engineTurboTooltipDisabled: Unavailable: strategy contains heavy blocks (tape/orderbook) requiring Precision engine.
configPanel.deployDesc: Will launch a new instance of this strategy to trade on the symbol specified above.
configPanel.noActiveKeysWarning: No active API keys. Add and verify a key in Settings.

toasts.nameAndSymbolRequiredBacktest: Strategy name and symbol are required to run a backtest.
toasts.mustSaveBeforeDeploy: You must save the strategy before deploying it.
toasts.toastReset: All blocks and settings have been reset to their default values.

configPanel.enableMlConfirmationTooltip: If enabled, the strategy will require a confirmation from the ML model before entering a trade.
configPanel.symbolSelectionModeTooltip: This setting only applies to live trading.
configPanel.dynamicSelectionProOnly: Dynamic symbol selection is available for Pro users only.
configPanel.enableOracleFilterTooltip: Filter signals based on the current market regime predicted by the Oracle.

jsonEditor.title: JSON Configuration
jsonEditor.description: You can directly edit the JSON structure of the strategy here. Changes will be reflected in the visual editor after applying.
jsonEditor.toastApplySuccess: The strategy has been updated from the JSON configuration.

tooltip.explanation: Allows the strategy to operate only during specific UTC hours. Useful for trading during specific market sessions (London, New York, etc.).

params.name: Session
params.desc: Select the trading session during which the strategy will be active.

volatility_filter.title: Volatility Filter
volatility_filter.desc: Trade when ATR or BBW is above/below a value.

tooltip.explanation: Filters entry signals based on the current market volatility. Uses the ATR (Average True Range) or BBW (Bollinger Bands Width) indicator.

params.name: Indicator
params.desc: ATR for absolute volatility, BBW for relative volatility.

params.name: Operator
params.desc: Greater than (>) to trade in a volatile market, less than (<) to trade in a calm market.

params.name: Value
params.desc: The threshold value of the indicator for comparison.

trend_filter.title: Trend Filter
trend_filter.desc: Trade when ADX is above a value.

tooltip.explanation: Allows trading only when a strong trend is present, as determined by the ADX (Average Directional Index) indicator.

params.name: ADX (14)
params.desc: The trend strength indicator. A value above the threshold indicates a trend (either bullish or bearish).

params.name: Threshold
params.desc: A commonly accepted value for defining a trend is 25.

senior_tf_confluence.title: Senior TF Confluence
senior_tf_confluence.desc: Container for checks on a higher timeframe.

tooltip.explanation: This is a logical container. All conditions placed inside it will be checked against data from the higher timeframe specified in the parameters.

params.name: Timeframe
params.desc: Select the timeframe on which the nested conditions will be checked.

tooltip.explanation: Assesses if the market is 'alive' enough for trading. It checks two key metrics: normalized volatility (NATR) and volume spikes (Relative Volume).

params.name: NATR
params.desc: Normalized ATR (in %). Shows volatility relative to price. A value > 1.0% usually indicates good activity.

params.name: Rel.Vol
params.desc: Relative Volume. Shows how many times the current candle's volume is higher than the average. A value > 1.5-2.0 indicates a spike in interest.

natr_filter.title: NATR Filter
natr_filter.desc: Checks if NATR is above a certain threshold.

tooltip.explanation: Filters signals based on normalized volatility (NATR). Allows trading only when the market is active enough.

params.name: NATR Threshold
params.desc: The minimum NATR value (in %) required to pass the filter. A value > 1.0 usually indicates good activity.

rel_vol_filter.title: Relative Volume Filter
rel_vol_filter.desc: Checks if relative volume is above a threshold.

tooltip.explanation: Filters signals based on a spike in trading volume. Compares the current candle's volume to the average volume.

params.name: Rel.Vol Threshold
params.desc: The multiplier for the average volume. A value > 1.5 indicates a significant volume spike.

order_book_zone.title: Order Book Zone Analysis
order_book_zone.desc: Analyzes volume in a specified price range from the current price

l2_microstructure_check.title: L2 Microstructure
l2_microstructure_check.desc: Detailed analysis of the full L2 book for spoofing, icebergs etc. Best for final triggers.

tooltip.explanation: Detailed analysis of the full L2 book for spoofing, icebergs etc. Best for final triggers.

params.name: Check Type
params.desc: The type of L2 microstructure check to perform.

params.name: Single Order Size USD
params.desc: The size of a single large order to look for.

tooltip.explanation: Checks if the current price is near key levels like the previous day's or week's High/Low. Proximity is determined as a fraction of ATR.

params.name: Level Type
params.desc: Select the specific level (Daily High, Daily Low, etc.) to check against.

round_level.title: Round Level
round_level.desc: Proximity to a round number price.

tooltip.explanation: Checks if the price is near a 'round' number (e.g., 100.00, 1.5000, 0.75000). Such levels often act as psychological barriers.

params.name: Proximity (pips)
params.desc: The allowable distance to the round level in pips (minimum price increments).

tooltip.explanation: Determines the global trend direction based on the price's position relative to a long-period moving average (typically EMA 200).

params.name: Source
params.desc: The type of moving average (EMA/SMA).

params.name: Period
params.desc: The averaging period. 200 is a standard for defining the long-term trend.

tooltip.explanation: Checks if an entry signal was confirmed by a spike in trading volume. The condition is true if the signal candle's volume exceeds the average volume over the last N candles by a specified multiplier.

params.name: Multiplier
params.desc: How many times the volume must exceed the average.

local_level.title: Local Level
local_level.is_data_provider_label: Use as data provider (disable proximity check)

tooltip.explanation: Finds local highs and lows over a specified period on the selected timeframe and checks if the current price is near one of these levels.

params.name: Timeframe
params.desc: The timeframe to search for levels.

params.name: Lookback bars
params.desc: The number of recent candles to analyze.

params.name: Level type
params.desc: Use local highs, local lows, or both.

params.name: Proximity type
params.desc: How to measure proximity: in ATR multipliers or as a percentage of the price.

params.name: Proximity value
params.desc: The value for the ATR multiplier or percentage.

tooltip.defaults: On 15m TF, for 20 bars, High level, proximity ATR x 0.25

tape_analysis.title: Tape Analysis
tape_analysis.desc: Calculates tape activity metrics over a specified period

tooltip.explanation: Checks for the presence of one of the classic candlestick patterns on the last closed candle of the working timeframe.

params.name: Pattern Name
params.desc: Select a pattern from the list to check for.

tooltip.explanation: Checks if the price is in a consolidation phase (range-bound market) by comparing the range of the last N candles to the current ATR.

params.name: Lookback Period
params.desc: The number of recent candles to check for consolidation.

params.name: Max Range (ATR)
params.desc: The maximum allowed range (High - Low) for the lookback period, expressed as a multiplier of the current ATR. A smaller value means a tighter consolidation.

btc_state_filter.title: BTC State
btc_state_filter.desc: Filter by BTC's trend or range.

tooltip.explanation: Allows filtering altcoin signals based on the current state of the BTC market. Helps avoid entering trades against the 'market leader'.

params.name: Required State
params.desc: Select the BTC state during which trading is allowed.

tooltip.explanation: Uses the dynamics of Open Interest as an additional foundation for entry. For example, a rise in OI along with a price increase confirms trend strength.

params.name: Analyze
params.desc: What to check: the percentage change in OI or its absolute value.

params.name: Lookback Period
params.desc: The number of recent candles for analysis.

params.name: Operator
params.desc: Greater than (>) or less than (<).

params.name: Value
params.desc: The threshold value for comparison.

tooltip.explanation: Filters signals if the current instrument is too strongly or weakly correlated with BTC. Helps find instruments that move independently of the main market.

params.name: Lookback Period
params.desc: The number of recent candles to calculate the correlation.

params.name: Operator
params.desc: Greater than (>) or less than (<).

params.name: Value
params.desc: The threshold value for the correlation coefficient (from -1 to 1).

ma_cross_condition.title: MA Cross
ma_cross_condition.desc: When a fast MA crosses a slow MA.

tooltip.explanation: The condition becomes true when a 'fast' moving average crosses a 'slow' moving average from above or below.

params.name: Fast Period
params.desc: The period for the fast MA.

params.name: Slow Period
params.desc: The period for the slow MA.

params.name: Direction
params.desc: Crossover from above (buy signal) or from below (sell signal).

params.name: Shift
params.desc: The offset in candles to check (0 = current candle).

rsi_condition.title: RSI Condition
rsi_condition.desc: When RSI is above/below a value.

tooltip.explanation: Checks if the RSI (Relative Strength Index) indicator is in the overbought (usually > 70) or oversold (usually < 30) zone.

params.name: Period
params.desc: The RSI calculation period (standard is 14).

params.name: Operator
params.desc: Greater than (>) or less than (<).

params.name: Value
params.desc: The threshold value (e.g., 70 or 30).

params.name: Shift
params.desc: The offset in candles to check (0 = current candle).

value_comparison.title: Value Comparison
value_comparison.desc: Compares two dynamic or static values

tooltip.explanation: A universal block for comparing two values. These values can be static numbers, results from other blocks, candle prices, or position parameters. Allows creating flexible conditions, for example, 'Close Price > EMA(20)' or 'RSI < 30'.

params.name: Left Operand
params.desc: The first value for comparison. Can be a static number, the result of another block, a candle price, or a position parameter.

params.name: Operator
params.desc: Comparison type: greater than (>), less than (<), greater than or equal to (>=), less than or equal to (<=).

params.name: Right Operand
params.desc: The second value for comparison. Similar to the left operand.

macd_condition.title: MACD Condition
macd_condition.desc: MACD Line vs Signal Line or Histogram.

tooltip.explanation: Checks the state of the MACD indicator. It can track the crossover of the main and signal lines or the value of the histogram.

params.name: Periods
params.desc: Standard periods: 12 (fast), 26 (slow), 9 (signal).

params.name: Condition
params.desc: Select the type of MACD signal to track.

params.name: Shift
params.desc: The offset in candles to check (0 = current candle).

tooltip.defaults: MACD line crosses above Signal line

tooltip.explanation: Checks where the price closed relative to the upper or lower Bollinger Bands.

params.name: Price Source
params.desc: Which candle price to use (Close, High, Low).

params.name: Location
params.desc: Close above the upper band or below the lower band.

params.name: Shift
params.desc: The offset in candles to check (0 = current candle).

tooltip.defaults: Price Close closes above the Upper Band

tooltip.explanation: Checks the state of the Stochastic oscillator, including crossovers of the %K and %D lines or being in overbought/oversold zones.

params.name: Condition
params.desc: Select the type of Stochastic signal to track.

params.name: Level
params.desc: Used for 'greater than/less than level' conditions.

params.name: Shift
params.desc: The offset in candles to check (0 = current candle).

price_vs_level.title: Price vs Level
price_vs_level.desc: Compare price with a dynamic level.

tooltip.explanation: A universal block for comparing two values, where one can be dynamic (e.g., candle close price, another block's result, position entry price).

params.name: Source 1
params.desc: The first value to compare. Can be a candle price or another dynamic parameter.

params.name: Source 2
params.desc: The second value. You can link another block's result here (e.g., 'local_level') or a position parameter.

and.title: 'AND' Group
and.desc: All nested conditions must be true.

tooltip.explanation: A logical operator. The entire group condition will be true only if EVERY nested condition is true.

AND.title: 'AND' Group
AND.desc: All nested conditions must be true.

tooltip.explanation: A logical operator. The entire group condition will be true only if EVERY nested condition is true.

or.title: 'OR' Group
or.desc: At least one nested condition must be true.

tooltip.explanation: A logical operator. The entire group condition will be true if AT LEAST ONE of the nested conditions is true.

OR.title: 'OR' Group
OR.desc: At least one nested condition must be true.

tooltip.explanation: A logical operator. The entire group condition will be true if AT LEAST ONE of the nested conditions is true.

trailing_stop.title: Trailing Stop
trailing_stop.desc: Trail the stop loss behind the price.

tooltip.explanation: Automatically moves the stop loss to follow the price as it moves in a profitable direction. This helps to protect accumulated profits.

params.name: Type
params.desc: How to measure the distance from the price to the stop: in ATR multipliers or as a percentage.

params.name: Value
params.desc: The value of the ATR multiplier or percentage.

move_to_breakeven.title: Move to Breakeven
move_to_breakeven.desc: Remove risk after reaching a target.
move_to_breakeven.display_text: Move SL to Breakeven when profit reaches
move_to_breakeven.text_1: Move SL to Breakeven when profit reaches

tooltip.explanation: When a position reaches a certain profit level, this block automatically moves the stop loss to the entry price, making the trade risk-free.

params.name: Profit Target (in ATR)
params.desc: How many ATRs the price must move in profit for the stop loss to be moved to breakeven.

conditional_exit.title: Conditional Exit
conditional_exit.desc: Close position if conditions are met.
conditional_exit.display_text: Exit position if conditions are met
conditional_exit.text_1: Exit position if conditions are met:

tooltip.explanation: Allows defining a set of conditions (similar to entry conditions) that, when met, will immediately close the open position at the market price.

scale_in.title: Scale In / Add to Position
scale_in.desc: Increase position size if conditions are met.

tooltip.explanation: Allows adding to an already open, profitable position. Conditions for adding are placed inside this block.

params.name: Add Size
params.desc: The size of each new position chunk, expressed as a % of the initial risk. 100% means each new entry will have the same risk as the initial one.

params.name: Max Entries
params.desc: The total maximum number of entries for the position, including the very first one.

conditional_management.title: Conditional Management (IF/THEN)
conditional_management.desc: Execute an action if a condition is true.

tooltip.explanation: A powerful logic block for position management. IF the conditions in the left part become true, THEN the actions from the right part are executed. For example: IF PnL > 2%, THEN move stop loss to breakeven.

params.name: IF Zone
params.desc: A container for condition blocks that will be checked on every tick.

params.name: THEN Zone
params.desc: A container for action blocks (Modify SL, Trailing Stop) that will trigger once when the IF condition is met.

modify_stop_loss.title: Modify Stop Loss
modify_stop_loss.desc: Move SL to a new price.

tooltip.explanation: A direct action to modify a position's stop loss. Moves the SL to a specific new price. This price can be set statically or linked to a dynamic value (e.g., a level found by another block).

params.name: New SL Price
params.desc: The target price for the new stop loss.

modify_take_profit.title: Modify Take Profit
modify_take_profit.desc: Move TP to a new price.

tooltip.explanation: A direct action to modify a position's take profit. Moves the TP to a specific new price. This price can be set statically or linked to a dynamic value.

params.name: New TP Price
params.desc: The target price for the new take profit.

tooltip.explanation: Immediately closes the open position at the current market price when triggered.

dca_management.title: DCA Management
dca_management.desc: Automatic position averaging via martingale or custom conditions.

tooltip.explanation: Allows automatic position averaging when price moves against you, improving the average entry price. Supports limit grids (by % or ATR) with step multiplier option, and averaging based on custom visual conditions.

params.name: SO Orders
params.desc: Maximum number of safety orders.

params.name: Vol. Mult
params.desc: Volume multiplier for each subsequent order (martingale).

params.name: Step
params.desc: Step type between orders (% from price, ATR, or custom conditions).

params.name: Step Mult
params.desc: Step multiplier. Increases the distance between each subsequent safety order.

grid_management.title: GRID Management
grid_management.desc: Place a grid of limit orders within a specified range.

tooltip.explanation: Places a grid of limit orders within a specified price range. Allows for gradual entry or exit from a position.

params.name: Levels
params.desc: Number of levels in the grid.

params.name: Range
params.desc: How to define the range (%, ATR, or fixed prices).

params.name: Bounds
params.desc: Upper and lower bounds of the grid.

tooltip.explanation: Composite block for analyzing real-time trade tape. Allows checking various tape activity metrics (e.g., volume delta, buy/sell ratio) over a specified time interval.

params.name: Metric
params.desc: Select the metric for tape analysis.

params.name: Operator
params.desc: Comparison operator (>, <, >=, <=).

params.name: Value
params.desc: Threshold value for the metric.

params.name: Period (sec)
params.desc: Time window in seconds for aggregating tape data.

tooltip.defaults: Check Total Volume ($) > multiplier of average x2 for 5s

order_book_zone_condition.title: Order Book Condition
order_book_zone_condition.desc: Checks liquidity density in the order book.

tooltip.explanation: Composite block for analyzing liquidity density in the exchange order book. Allows checking total volume, largest level, or number of levels within a specified range (in percentage, ATR multipliers, or ticks) from the current price.

params.name: Metric
params.desc: Select the metric for order book analysis.

params.name: Side
params.desc: Which side of the order book to analyze (bids or asks).

params.name: Range Value
params.desc: The value of the range for analysis.

params.name: Range Type
params.desc: How to define the range: in %, ATR multipliers, or ticks.

params.name: Operator
params.desc: Comparison operator (>, <, >=, <=).

params.name: Value
params.desc: Threshold value for the metric.

tooltip.defaults: Total Volume ($) in bids within range 2% > 1000000

level_proximity_condition.title: Level Proximity Condition
level_proximity_condition.desc: Checks price proximity to a local level.

tooltip.explanation: Composite block for checking price proximity to a local level (High/Low) on a given timeframe over a specified number of bars. Proximity is measured in ATR multipliers or percentages.

params.name: Price Source
params.desc: Which price to use for checking (Close, High, Low).

params.name: Level Timeframe
params.desc: The timeframe on which the level is searched.

params.name: Lookback Bars
params.desc: The number of recent candles to search for the level.

params.name: Proximity Value
params.desc: The value of proximity (ATR multiplier or percentage).

params.name: Proximity Type
params.desc: How to measure proximity: in ATR multipliers or percentages.

tooltip.defaults: Close price near level on 5m TF for 20 bars within 0.25 x ATR

tradingview_signal.title: TradingView Signal
tradingview_signal.desc: Awaits a TradingView webhook with a specific ID.

tooltip.explanation: This block allows you to use external signals from TradingView as part of your logic. When a webhook arrives with a matching ID, this block becomes 'True' for the specified number of seconds.

params.name: Signal ID
params.desc: A unique identifier that you will specify in your TradingView webhook message.

params.name: TTL
params.desc: How long the signal remains active after receiving the webhook (in seconds).

params.name: Weight
params.desc: The weight of this signal if you are using weighted foundation logic.

tooltip.explanation: Detects a squeeze by comparing the range of the first half of the lookback window with the current half.

params.name: Lookback
params.desc: Number of candles to analyze.

params.name: Ratio
params.desc: Current range must be below this share of the past range.

tooltip.explanation: Analyzes market structure by finding sequences of Higher Highs / Higher Lows (bullish) or Lower Highs / Lower Lows (bearish).

params.name: Structure Type
params.desc: The trendline structure to look for.

params.name: Lookback
params.desc: Number of candles to search for extremes.

params.name: Points
params.desc: Minimum extrema points required.

params.name: Order
params.desc: Number of candles on each side to confirm a fractal (extreme).

tooltip.defaults: Structure: Higher Lows, Lookback: 30, Points: 2, Order: 3

level_touch_analyzer.title: Level Touch Analyzer
level_touch_analyzer.desc: Precise touch, breakout, or retest of a level.

tooltip.explanation: Counts how many times price touched a detected level and can invalidate the pattern after a deep wick pierce.

params.name: Level Source
params.desc: Dynamic link to a level provider such as local_level.

params.name: Lookback
params.desc: Number of candles to scan.

params.name: Tolerance
params.desc: Touch zone as percent of level price.

params.name: Touches
params.desc: Minimum touches required.

return_to_level.title: Return to Level
return_to_level.desc: Awaits price return to a previously defined level.

tooltip.explanation: Monitors price return to a level defined by another block (e.g., local or significant level). Supports simple touch or full 'breakout-retest' scenarios.

params.name: Level Source
params.desc: Dynamic reference to the block that found the level.

params.name: Retest Type
params.desc: Touch (immediate) or Breakout-Retest (requires price to move away from level first).

params.name: Direction
params.desc: Where the price should come from to activate.

params.name: Confirmation Time
params.desc: How many seconds the price must stay in the level zone.

params.name: Proximity Type & Value
params.desc: Touch zone threshold (in ATR or % of price). Higher values make it easier to trigger a touch.

params.name: Departure Type & Value
params.desc: For breakout mode: how far price must move away before returning (in ATR or % of price).

tooltip.defaults: Type: Touch, Direction: Any, Confirmation: 5s, Prox: 0.1, Dept: 1.5

ai.title: AI Co-pilot
ai.description: Describe your trading idea in plain language. The AI will build the foundation of your strategy.
placeholders: Create a simple RSI strategy on the 5-minute chart.
Build a breakout strategy for ETHUSDT that uses an ATR-based trailing stop.
I need a trend-following strategy with a 200 EMA filter on the hourly chart.
ai.compactPlaceholder: e.g., 'add a trailing stop by ATR'
ai.disclaimer: AI Assistant can make mistakes. Verify the generated output. This is not financial advice.

step1.title: Welcome to the Editor!
step1.content: This is your canvas for creating trading strategies. Let's start by naming your strategy in the panel on the right.

step2.title: Component Palette
step2.content: On the left is the palette with all the building blocks. Open the 'Indicators' section to find the block we need.

step3.title: Adding Logic
step3.content: Great! Now drag the 'RSI' block onto the entry canvas. This will be the condition for our purchase.

step4.title: Configuring the Condition
step4.content: Let's set up the buy rule. We want to buy when the market is 'oversold'. Set the condition: RSI less than 30.

step5.title: Entry Foundation
step5.content: Now let's add a 'Foundation'. Drag the 'Local Level' block to the dedicated area for foundations.

step6.title: Running a Backtest
step6.content: Your first strategy is ready! Let's test it on historical data. Click 'Run Backtest'.

step7.title: Congratulations!
step7.content: You have created and tested your first strategy! You can now continue to refine it or proceed to analyze the results.

finalDialog.title: Congratulations! Your first backtest is running!
finalDialog.description: You will be redirected to the 'Research' page to view the results. If you have any questions about creating more complex strategies, feel free to ask our AI assistant!

welcomeDialog.title: Welcome to the Strategy Editor!
welcomeDialog.description: Let's create your first trading strategy together. It will only take a couple of minutes.

calculator.title: DCA Projection & Calculator
calculator.warningMaxDrop: Warning: Max drop exceeds 100%. Price cannot fall below zero.
calculator.warningExchangeMin: Warning: Some orders are below $5 (typical exchange minimum).

gridCalculator.title: GRID Projection & Calculator
gridCalculator.warningNegativeProfitDesc: Grid step is too small. Exchange fees will consume all profits.
